extra.message: SQL injection vulnerability: User input 'code' parameter is directly concatenated into SQL query using f-string without parameterized query. The variable 'code' comes from form.registration_code.data (line 45) and is interpolated directly into the SQL string at line 17, allowing an attacker to break out of the query and execute arbitrary SQL.

SQL injection vulnerability: User input 'search' parameter from request.args.get() is directly concatenated into SQL LIKE clause using f-string without parameterization. The search_param variable (line 27) is interpolated directly into text() query (line 33), allowing SQL injection attacks.

Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) vulnerability: User-controlled error object and request.path are directly rendered using render_template_string() with an f-string template. This allows an attacker to inject Jinja2 template expressions via the URL path to execute arbitrary code.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: Insecure deserialization vulnerability: User-controlled 'preferences' cookie value is decoded from base64 and deserialized using pickle.loads() without validation. An attacker can craft a malicious pickle payload to achieve remote code execution.

Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability: User-supplied URL from form.url.data (routes/account.py line 61) is directly passed to urlopen() without validation or URL scheme/domain restrictions. An attacker can provide internal URLs (e.g., http://localhost, http://169.254.169.254) to access internal resources.

Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) / Missing Authorization: Note deletion endpoint at /notes/<note_id>/delete does not verify that the current user owns the note before deletion. The endpoint only checks if the note exists (line 44-45), not if the note belongs to the current user. An attacker can delete any note by crafting a direct request.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: Hardcoded secret key vulnerability: Flask secret key is hardcoded as 'super secret key' in the source code (line 11). This key is used for session management and CSRF protection. If source code is compromised, attackers can forge sessions and bypass CSRF protections. Should use environment variables.

Privilege escalation vulnerability: The account update endpoint at /account (line 68-95) allows users to modify arbitrary attributes via form.data.items() (lines 76-80). While it attempts to exclude 'password', the form includes an 'is_admin' field (forms/account_form.py line 8) that is not explicitly excluded. An attacker can set is_admin=True to escalate their privileges to admin.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) / Missing Authorization: The /accounts/<user_id>/notes endpoint (line 41-48) does not verify that the requesting user has permission to view the specified user_id's notes. It retrieves all notes for the given user_id without any ownership check, allowing any authenticated user to view any other user's notes by manipulating the user_id parameter.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability: Note text is rendered with the 'safe' filter (line 31: '{{ note.text | safe }}'), which disables HTML escaping. Combined with CKEditor (which allows rich HTML), an attacker can inject malicious JavaScript that executes in other users' browsers when they view the note.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation
